channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Steelers owner Art Rooney II sees 'positive signs' that Aaron Rodgers will join the team
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Steelers owner Art Rooney II says there are “positive signs” that Aaron Rodgers will play in Pittsburgh. The four-time NFL MVP worked out recently with new Steelers wide receiver DK Metcalf after previously visiting the team.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL postpones a decision on the tush push but passes other rule changes
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The future of the tush push has been pushed until next month. NFL team owners had been set to vote Tuesday on Green Bay’s proposal to ban the play that’s helped the Philadelphia Eagles win one Super Bowl and reach another, but the proposal was tabled until May.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA suspends 5 players for their roles in Pistons-Timberwolves altercation that spilled into stands
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Detroit's Isaiah Stewart was suspended for two games and four other players from the Pistons and Minnesota Timberwolves drew one-game suspensions for their roles in an on-court altercation between the teams earlier this week, the NBA said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Super Bowl champ Richard Sherman joins long list of sports figures whose homes have been burglarized
item.description: MAPLE VALLEY, Calif. (AP) — Former NFL player Richard Sherman is the latest sports figure whose home has been burglarized. The Super Bowl champion posted pictures and a video on social media asking if anyone recognized three armed intruders that broke into his house last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No stigma for Bueckers, Van Lith or Fudd. The basketball stars seek out mental health professionals
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Paige Bueckers, Hailey Van Lith and Azzi Fudd all have reached out to a mental health professional for help at one point, seeking assistance to cope with the increasing pressure on college basketball players.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Browns' trade for Deshaun Watson was 'big swing and miss,' team owner says
item.description: Cleveland Browns co-owner Jimmy Haslam expressed some remorse for trading for Deshaun Watson and giving him a lucrative extension amid the team's woes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-Canada-Mexico joint World Cup goes from unity to acrimony thanks to tariffs and '51st state' talk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Seven years ago, when a joint bid by the United States, Canada and Mexico was awarded the 2026 World Cup, rifts created by tariffs — yes, back then, too! — and a proposed border wall were glossed over because of the neighbors’ longstanding political and economic alliances.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Caitlin Clark being 'White girl from the middle of America' helped connect with young fans, ESPN pundit says
item.description: ESPN basketball analyst Monica McNutt suggested Caitlin Clark being a "White girl from the middle of America" played into her connection with young girls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In recruiting battle, lawmakers weigh whether to give college athletes a tax break on NIL earnings
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Illinois state Rep. Travis Weaver remembers how surreal it felt having Heisman Trophy winner Mark Ingram sit a few rows behind him in a political science class. He will never forget the pure joy in Alabama every time the Crimson Tide rolled to the national championship, either.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zion Williamson's season is over, as Pelicans shut down the forward and CJ McCollum
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Zion Williamson and CJ McCollum will miss the rest of the season because of injuries, the New Orleans Pelicans said Monday. Williamson sustained a low back bone contusion after a fall during a game on March 19 in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milwaukee pays tribute to Bob Uecker in Brewers' first home game since his death
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — As the Milwaukee Brewers conducted a moment of silence before their home opener to honor Bob Uecker, one fan decided keeping quiet wasn’t the best way to pay tribute to the lovable loquacious broadcaster. “We love you, Ueck,” the spectator shouted.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stanford hires Frank Reich as interim football coach
item.description: STANFORD, Calif. (AP) — Andrew Luck picked a familiar face to take over as Stanford's interim football coach, hiring former NFL coach Frank Reich on Monday to replace the fired Troy Taylor.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dan Mullen, awed by UNLV facilities, says timing felt right to make return to coaching
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Dan Mullen could've returned to coaching sooner, but the timing never seemed right and, regardless, he was fine remaining at ESPN giving his insights about the latest in college football.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lee Cummard named BYU women's basketball coach
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU has named Lee Cummard as its next women's basketball head coach. Cummard has been the interim head coach since Amber Whiting resigned after three seasons on March 8.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DePaul women's basketball coach Doug Bruno steps down after 39 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Chicago has produced a lot of basketball icons and one of them in Lincoln Park is stepping down — DePaul's longtime women's basketball coach Doug Bruno.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: RSL seeks redemption in Minnesota after home setback
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4) — Real Salt Lake looks to bounce back from a rare home loss as they go on the road to face Minnesota United FC on Saturday night. Real Salt Lake’s attack will be bolstered by the return of Polish attacking winger Dominic Marzcuk and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Will Applegate: Why Grand Junction's three-sport star chose baseball and his legacy on GJHS
item.description: Few local high school athletes have made an impact quite like Grand Junction High School's Will Applegate.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: San Diego State Aztec guard declares for NBA Draft
item.description: San Diego State Aztecs men's basketball player Miles Byrd is looking to make his next move to the professional level.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Tony Finau out to win second Houston Open
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Utah native Tony Finau came into this year after going through two pretty big life events. The first was knee surgery back in December.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: Warriors' Stephen Curry won't play in Miami, 2nd straight game missed with pelvic contusion
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Stephen Curry is going to take a little more time to heal. Curry remained out of Golden State's lineup Tuesday night for a game against the Miami Heat, the second straight game he has missed with a pelvic contusion.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4
